rationale: The citing paper uses Dhar and Wertenbroch (2000) to ground a core conceptual distinction in its theory section: hedonic versus utilitarian product types and their link to pleasure and guilt. That distinction is not a peripheral aside; it is incorporated into the paper's purchase-context pillar and tested as a moderator in the meta-analytic model. Although the citation is brief, it supports a specific theoretical mechanism used to structure hypotheses and interpretation.
relation_description: The citing meta-analysis draws on Dhar and Wertenbroch (2000) as theoretical grounding for the hedonic-versus-utilitarian product framework. It then applies that framework as a substantive moderator of cause-related marketing effectiveness.
